routes.title: ComplyEaze | Choose the right compliance surface
routes.description: Compare the public job, evidence, and custody boundary for Axal, Pack, and Tools before choosing where to continue.
routes.heading: Choose the work. See the boundary. Check the evidence.
routes.summary: ComplyEaze helps CA firms, compliance teams, and business operators route work to Axal for coordinated practice review, Pack for local GST evidence collection, or Tools for browser-local utility work.

sections.title: Coordinate practice work with Axal
sections.body: Use the authenticated workspace when matters, obligations, evidence, clients, documents, and tasks need permission-aware coordination and human review.

sections.title: Collect GST evidence locally with Pack
sections.body: Use the browser extension for the narrow filed-return collection job when local artifact control and source provenance matter more than shared workspace custody.

sections.title: Create quick browser-local artifacts with Tools
sections.body: Use the public utilities for focused review work from pasted rows or plain text when no account, upload, or saved tenant context is needed.

routes.title: Products | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Compare Axal, Pack, and Tools by job, public evidence, custody boundary, and current public posture.
routes.heading: Choose by job and custody boundary
routes.summary: The products share a trust posture, not a single workflow. Start with the work you need to complete, then verify where context lives and which public evidence supports the product description.

sections.title: ComplyEaze is the public trust layer
sections.body: This site explains the family, its public evidence, and the boundary between inspectable product information and authenticated operations.

sections.title: Different jobs need different custody
sections.body: Axal keeps permission-aware firm context, Pack keeps its narrow evidence job local to the browser, and Tools creates browser-local drafts without account state.

sections.title: Evidence comes before reliance
sections.body: Review the linked public source and current status before relying on security, release, browser-local, integration, or compliance-related product statements.

products.name: Axal
products.job: Coordinate matters, obligations, evidence, clients, documents, and tasks for CA practice teams.
products.proof: The public route family documents workspace-first, permission-aware, source-led compliance review concepts.
products.boundary: Account, workspace, role, and saved operational context belong to the authenticated product.
products.status: Authenticated workspace; public marketing preview limited

products.name: Pack
products.job: Collect filed-return evidence for a narrow GST Portal task while retaining local artifact control.
products.proof: Pack owns its public extension source, release evidence, privacy posture, security policy, and support facts.
products.boundary: Browser extension and local artifacts; no shared workspace custody or backend-upload claim.
products.status: Public extension; current release evidence owned by Pack

products.name: Tools
products.job: Create focused review artifacts from pasted rows or plain text without opening an account.
products.proof: The Tools repository owns the utility registry, browser-local runtime, release facts, and source evidence.
products.boundary: Browser-local drafts; no account, upload, tenant state, or saved operational context.

routes.title: Pack | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Understand Pack's local-first GST evidence role, custody boundary, and product-owned public proof before continuing.
routes.heading: Keep the evidence job local. Verify the product proof.
routes.summary: Pack is the ComplyEaze-family browser-extension lane for authorised users collecting selected GST Portal evidence into their own browser environment. Pack owns the current capability, release, permission, privacy, and security evidence.

sections.title: Use Pack for a focused evidence task
sections.body: Pack's public source describes a local-first browser-extension workflow for authorised users collecting selected filed-return artifacts from their own GST Portal session. Exact supported artifacts and release state must be checked on Pack-owned surfaces.

sections.title: Where Pack data stays
sections.body: Pack owns its browser runtime and local extension storage. This ComplyEaze gateway does not receive portal credentials, session material, taxpayer artifacts, downloaded files, or extension state.

sections.title: What to check before using Pack
sections.body: Use the product-owned links below to verify Pack's current source, release, privacy, and security posture.

evidenceLinks.label: Review Pack source
evidenceLinks.description: Inspect the public extension source and its documented privacy invariants.

evidenceLinks.label: Review Pack releases
evidenceLinks.description: Check the current public release artifacts and notes before relying on distribution or capability claims.

evidenceLinks.label: Review Pack privacy
evidenceLinks.description: Read the product-owned privacy boundary for the public Pack surface.

evidenceLinks.label: Review Pack security
evidenceLinks.description: Read the public vulnerability-reporting and security-maintenance policy.

routes.title: Tools | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Understand the browser-local Tools job, custody boundary, and product-owned public proof before choosing a utility.
routes.heading: Create a local draft. Keep professional review in the loop.
routes.summary: Tools is the ComplyEaze-family lane for browser-local first-pass compliance drafts created from pasted rows or plain text. The Tools host owns each utility's runtime, input, output, accessibility, privacy, and release evidence.

sections.title: Use Tools for a first-pass draft
sections.body: Tools provides focused browser-local utilities for reconciliation triage, payables review, follow-up drafts, evidence memos, and review copies. Each output remains a draft for human and professional review.

sections.title: Where your inputs and drafts stay
sections.body: The public Tools source says files are processed in the browser without an account or file upload. Product hosting and security controls may still process ordinary browser and security signals.

sections.title: What to check before using Tools
sections.body: Use the product-owned links below to verify the current Tools source, privacy, security, and release gates.

evidenceLinks.label: Review Tools source
evidenceLinks.description: Inspect the utility registry, browser-local implementation, and public limitations.

evidenceLinks.label: Review Tools privacy
evidenceLinks.description: Read the product-owned privacy boundary and hosting qualification.

evidenceLinks.label: Review Tools release gates
evidenceLinks.description: Inspect the checks required before a utility or public claim is released.

evidenceLinks.label: Review Tools security
evidenceLinks.description: Read the public vulnerability-reporting and security-maintenance policy.

routes.title: About ComplyEaze
routes.description: How ComplyEaze separates public product proof from authenticated compliance operations.
routes.heading: A public surface for proof, not private operations
routes.summary: ComplyEaze explains its product family and the evidence required before public claims, route migrations, or private-app cleanup move forward.

sections.title: Why this public boundary exists
sections.body: Inspectable website, trust, migration, release, and product-family pages belong outside the private application that handles authenticated work.

sections.title: Different products, different jobs
sections.body: Axal supports practice operations, Pack supports browser-local evidence collection, Tools supports browser-local utility work, and Sanchika proves shared design contracts.

sections.title: Boundaries remain visible
sections.body: Public storytelling must not blur into tenant custody, portal sessions, filing certainty, credential handling, or private production operations.

proof: Open-source policies define contribution, security, support, trademark, and review expectations.
Rendered pages are checked for metadata, links, public claims, and desktop, tablet, and mobile behavior.
Private-app cleanup remains blocked until hosted route, canonical, redirect, and rollback evidence exists.

routes.title: Contact routing | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Choose the safe channel for public-site feedback, security reports, and private product inquiries.
routes.heading: Pick the channel before sharing details
routes.summary: Public website feedback belongs in the public repository, but customer, taxpayer, credential, document, and portal details must stay out of public issues.

sections.title: Public page issue
sections.body: Use public issues for broken links, typos, accessibility problems, route migration gaps, or source-backed claim corrections that contain only synthetic data.

sections.title: Sensitive report
sections.body: Use the private vulnerability reporting path for suspected secrets, sensitive-data exposure, misleading collection behavior, or security issues.

sections.title: Product or customer inquiry
sections.body: Do not put client, taxpayer, credential, document, phone, or portal details into this public repository. Use the established private ComplyEaze channel instead.

proof: No contact form or submission API is copied into this static route.
Phone numbers, office addresses, and named individual profile links are not embedded by default.
Public issues should contain synthetic reproduction steps and redacted evidence only.

routes.title: Trust and safety | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Public safety rules and source evidence for ComplyEaze claims, screenshots, dependencies, and review practice.
routes.heading: Public claims need evidence
routes.summary: This repository is open by design, so trust comes from narrow claims, synthetic examples, explicit product boundaries, and public source evidence rather than implied authority.

sections.title: Synthetic evidence only
sections.body: Screenshots, fixtures, demos, and issue reports must avoid real taxpayer identifiers, documents, credentials, portal responses, customer details, and production screenshots.

sections.title: Source before confidence
sections.body: Security, privacy, release, browser-local, dependency, and statutory claims must point to source, release, runtime, or official evidence before a professional relies on them.

sections.title: Reserved marks and roles
sections.body: The source license does not grant rights to imply endorsement, affiliation, certification, government approval, or official ComplyEaze operation.

evidenceLinks.label: Security policy
evidenceLinks.description: Private reporting path and sensitive-data boundaries for public repository issues.

evidenceLinks.label: Public claim policy
evidenceLinks.description: Evidence requirements and prohibited public readiness or endorsement claims.

evidenceLinks.label: Dependency policy
evidenceLinks.description: Reviewed package, lockfile, workflow, lifecycle-script, and action-pinning boundaries.

evidenceLinks.label: Repository settings
evidenceLinks.description: Protected-check, review-gate, Pages-guard, and repository posture evidence.

evidenceLinks.label: Review and rectify
evidenceLinks.description: Required local and current-head review loop for meaningful public changes.

routes.title: Public runbooks | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Curated migration, release, visual-review, public-claim, and review runbooks for the ComplyEaze public site.
routes.heading: Follow the evidence path before changing a route
routes.summary: These source documents explain how public pages move, what evidence a release needs, and which conditions must remain blocked until production cutover is proven.

sections.title: Before commit
sections.body: Run the focused checks first, then lint, typecheck, tests, builds, visual review, public-content, links, metadata, and whitespace checks.

sections.title: Before cleanup
sections.body: Do not remove a private-app public route until the destination owns production traffic and hosted, canonical, redirect, rollback, and cleanup-PR evidence is recorded.

sections.title: Before public claims
sections.body: If the repository cannot prove a claim, narrow or remove it. A preview, artifact, or screenshot does not establish hosted cutover or private-app health.

evidenceLinks.label: Migration plan
evidenceLinks.description: Public-surface extraction order, evidence requirements, and cleanup boundary.

evidenceLinks.label: Route migration ledger
evidenceLinks.description: Host-aware source, destination, evidence, redirect, cleanup, and rollback records.

evidenceLinks.label: Release gates
evidenceLinks.description: Required checks, artifacts, review state, and dependency evidence before merge.

evidenceLinks.label: Visual testing
evidenceLinks.description: Desktop, tablet, mobile, accessibility, overflow, focus, and screenshot review rules.

evidenceLinks.label: Public claim policy
evidenceLinks.description: Source and evidence standards for compliance, privacy, release, and product claims.

evidenceLinks.label: Review and rectify
evidenceLinks.description: Before-every-commit local review and current-head GitHub review requirements.

routes.title: Route migration ledger | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Host-aware public route migration evidence for moving ComplyEaze-family pages without premature private-app cleanup.
routes.heading: No cleanup before route evidence
routes.summary: Every public route family needs a destination, hosted evidence, redirect decision, cleanup rule, and rollback path before a source route is removed or redirected.

sections.title: Family context
sections.body: Family summaries explain the intended public destination and product boundary without authorizing route cleanup.

sections.title: Route-level blockers
sections.body: Each source route keeps its own host, destination, evidence, redirect, cleanup PR, and rollback record so similarly named routes on different hosts cannot be confused.

sections.title: Evidence remains pending
sections.body: Preview builds, CI artifacts, and screenshots support review but do not prove production hosting, redirects, canonical behavior, or private-app cleanup readiness.

steps.label: Inventory
steps.body: Record the source host and route, intended destination, ownership boundary, and affected product family.

steps.label: Evidence
steps.body: Verify hosted response, canonical URL, sitemap intent, redirect behavior, visual output, public claims, and rollback evidence.

steps.label: Cleanup
steps.body: Remove or redirect the source only through a separate private-app cleanup PR after every required evidence field is reviewed.

routes.title: Privacy Boundary | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Privacy boundary for the ComplyEaze public website, trust, and migration evidence pages.
routes.heading: This repo does not process tenant data
routes.summary: The public site can be inspected, forked, and reviewed without crossing into authenticated app data, portal sessions, compliance documents, or private infrastructure.

sections.title: Repository boundary
sections.body: This public repository hosts website copy, static documentation, route ledgers, screenshots, and open-source governance files. It does not process tenant data, portal credentials, compliance documents, or authenticated Axal app workflows.

sections.title: Data used in this repo
sections.body: Examples, screenshots, fixtures, and issue reports must use synthetic data. Public evidence must not include PAN, GSTIN, Aadhaar, phone numbers, taxpayer names, portal HTML, notices, orders, credentials, OTPs, CAPTCHA images, cookies, tokens, downloaded files, local browser profiles, or production screenshots.

sections.title: Private app separation
sections.body: Authenticated services, workspace records, document processing, Prisma, Redis, workers, and portal automation stay outside this repository. Those systems need their own notices, controls, and review before their copy is migrated here.

sections.title: Contact and issues
sections.body: Use the repository support and security policies for public website, claim, or vulnerability reports. Do not place sensitive taxpayer, client, credential, document, or portal information in public issues or pull requests.

routes.title: Public Site Terms | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Terms for the ComplyEaze open website repository and its static public pages.
routes.heading: Open code is not an open trademark license
routes.summary: The website source is reviewable and Apache-licensed; the ComplyEaze-family marks, product claims, and professional-use boundaries remain controlled.

sections.title: Public-site use
sections.body: These terms describe use of the open public website repository and its static public pages. They are not a substitute for private product terms, customer agreements, professional advice, or third-party portal terms.

sections.title: Open-source license
sections.body: Source code is provided under the Apache License 2.0 unless a file says otherwise. Brand names, logos, and marks are reserved separately and are not granted by the source license.

sections.title: No official endorsement
sections.body: ComplyEaze public pages must not imply government approval, GSTN or CBIC endorsement, browser-store approval, statutory correctness, or production readiness unless the claim is backed by specific public evidence.

sections.title: Evidence before reliance
sections.body: Public content may summarize product direction, migration status, and release gates, but users must verify source, release, and professional evidence before relying on compliance, tax, accounting, legal, or filing decisions.

routes.title: Public Site Status | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Repository, rollback, and multi-app Astro build status for the ComplyEaze public site.
routes.heading: Static-site readiness, not app uptime
routes.summary: Status here means public repo readiness, migration posture, and release-gate evidence. Authenticated app health belongs to a separate operational channel.

policySummary.evidence: Astro build and aggregate rendered checks

sections.title: Current scope
sections.body: This status page covers the open public-site repository, static build gates, route migration readiness, and review evidence. It is not a private Axal app uptime page and does not report tenant service health.

sections.title: Build posture
sections.body: ComplyEaze, Axal, and Pack build independently as static noindex Astro apps from typed manifests. Release gates require lint, typecheck, tests, build, aggregate release evidence, visual screenshots, public-content checks, link checks, metadata checks, git diff --check, and review-thread clearance. Rollback is a Git revert backed by retained CI artifacts.

sections.title: Governance posture
sections.body: Legal governance, repository-settings gates, contributor-intake rules, dependency-surface policy, CODEOWNERS, and issue contact links are checked by scripts/check-public-repo.mjs so source-license, trademark, supply-chain, review, and public-issue boundaries cannot drift silently.

sections.title: Migration posture
sections.body: The typed manifests now own 15 ComplyEaze routes, six Axal routes, and one conservative Pack foundation. The old string renderer and its duplicate adapters are removed. Hosting and path routing remain pending, and route-level cleanup stays blocked until hosted routing, canonical URLs, redirect behavior, rollback evidence, and a separate private-app cleanup PR are reviewed.

sections.title: Hosting posture
sections.body: Pages deploy is gated and readiness-only behind ENABLE_GITHUB_PAGES_DEPLOY. A successful preview or artifact upload does not authorize cleanup because it is not production-host or parent-route cleanup evidence.

sections.title: Incident language
sections.body: A public status update in this repo must distinguish static-site build or content issues from authenticated app incidents. Do not publish customer, tenant, credential, portal, or production infrastructure details here.

routes.title: Public Changelog | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Source-backed changelog for ComplyEaze public-site migration slices.
routes.heading: Migration history stays auditable
routes.summary: Each public slice records what moved, what stayed blocked, and which evidence must exist before parent routes are cleaned up.

sections.title: 2026-07-16 - Sanchika v0.1 public adoption
sections.body: Adopted the exact reviewed Sanchika v0.1.0 GitHub release artifacts across the ComplyEaze, Axal, and Pack public Astro shells, added typed adoption and rollback evidence, expanded release evidence to 22 routes and 66 visual cells, and kept pattern composition, hosting, indexing, private-app changes, and Tools outside the tranche.

sections.title: 2026-07-16 - Astro manifest cutover
sections.body: Made the three typed manifests and their Astro outputs the only route and rendering sources, added aggregate release evidence for 21 routes, retained a 63-cell visual matrix, removed the duplicate string renderer, and kept deployment, routing, indexing, and private-app cleanup outside the change.

sections.title: 2026-07-16 - Axal six-route preview port
sections.body: Moved the Axal homepage and five customer workflow pages into one typed Axal manifest and the Axal Astro preview, preserved the established public identity, replaced the private product screenshot with a labelled synthetic workbench, and kept authentication, hosting, path routing, indexing, and private cleanup outside the tranche.

sections.title: 2026-07-16 - Product gateway route port
sections.body: Moved /products/pack/ and /products/tools/ into the canonical typed manifest and ComplyEaze Astro preview, routed product-family choices through customer-safe gateways, and kept product capability and release evidence on product-owned public surfaces.

sections.title: 2026-07-15 - Customer-core route port
sections.body: Moved / and /products/ into the canonical typed manifest and ComplyEaze Astro preview, replaced repository-boundary customer copy with job, custody, and evidence guidance, and retained the then-current rollback baseline.

sections.title: 2026-07-15 - Core public routes ported to Astro
sections.body: Moved /about/ and /contact/ into the canonical typed manifest and ComplyEaze Astro preview while retaining the then-current rollback baseline and proving rendered parity.

sections.title: 2026-07-15 - Multi-app Astro workspace
sections.body: Added static Astro foundations for ComplyEaze, Axal, and Pack plus private shared content and shell packages. The previews remain noindex and do not claim hosted cutover.

sections.title: 2026-07-15 - Review workflow retuning
sections.body: Retuned the findings-based Review gate while preserving unresolved-thread, requested-changes, before-every-commit local Codex review, and current-head GitHub Codex review requirements.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Dependency surface policy
sections.body: Extended scripts/public-checks/dependency-policy.mjs so package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, Dependabot update blocks, workflow pnpm and Node versions, lifecycle scripts, runtime dependencies, and pinned external GitHub Actions stay aligned with the public static-site trust boundary.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Contributor intake governance
sections.body: Expanded CODEOWNERS and issue contact links for owner-reviewed governance paths, private security and sensitive-data reports, conduct reports, and support boundaries, then added scripts/public-checks/contributor-intake.mjs to keep those intake guardrails enforced.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Repository settings gates
sections.body: Added scripts/public-checks/repository-settings.mjs, aligned pnpm verify and CI with git diff --check, pinned protected-check names, Pages guard prerequisites, review-gate posture, and custom-domain cutover blockers.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Legal governance checks
sections.body: Added scripts/public-checks/legal-governance.mjs so Apache-2.0 source-license boundaries, ComplyEaze-family trademark reservations, anti-endorsement language, fork/operator clarity, and contributor safety templates remain enforced.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Route cleanup governance
sections.body: Added route-cleanup issue intake, parent-route impact prompts, PR-template cutover checks, and static public checks so cleanup requests require hosted, redirect, rollback, ledger, and separate private-app cleanup PR evidence.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Route-level migration ledger
sections.body: Expanded the migration ledger from family summaries to source-route rows, rendered those blockers on /migration/, and added checks for duplicate routes, pending evidence, blocked cleanup, markdown alignment, and private-app PR requirements.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Hosted route cutover checks
sections.body: Added scripts/check-hosted-routes.mjs and the hosting cutover runbook so reviewers can verify hosted destination routes, robots, sitemap, canonical tags, and preview-vs-production origin boundaries against retained local release evidence without enabling Pages by default.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Public policy/status/release routes
sections.body: Seeded public-repo-specific privacy, terms, status, changelog, and release-evidence routes with local policy checks and visual coverage. Parent legal/contact/about pages remain cleanup-blocked pending separate review.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Axal marketing route family
sections.body: Seeded the Axal product index and five static Axal detail pages with narrowed public claims, dedicated checks, and desktop/tablet/mobile screenshots. Parent cleanup remains blocked until hosted route evidence exists.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Migration ledger and route evidence model
sections.body: Added the rendered migration ledger, route-family data, release gates, and review-gate semantics so future cleanup depends on evidence rather than assumption.

sections.title: 2026-07-08 - Open-source governance baseline
sections.body: Established AGENTS.md, contribution policy, security/support files, Apache 2.0 source license, trademark reservation, issue templates, PR template, CI, and review-gate workflow.

routes.title: Release Evidence | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Release evidence categories for ComplyEaze public-site changes.
routes.heading: No release without reviewable proof
routes.summary: A public-site change needs source checks, rendered screenshots, metadata, links, public-claim review, and a rollback story before private-route cleanup follows.

sections.title: Route manifest
sections.body: Three canonical typed route manifests feed the ComplyEaze, Axal, and Pack Astro apps. The root build validates all 22 actual HTML outputs and writes non-deployable aggregate evidence to test-results/public-build/route-manifest.json.

sections.title: Visual artifacts
sections.body: scripts/visual-check.mjs renders every public page at desktop, tablet, and mobile widths, writes screenshots under test-results/public-visual, and records reduced-motion state, focus targets, overflow, link, touch-target, image, blank-section, clipping, and first-viewport overlap evidence for review. CI retains this as the public-visual-evidence artifact.

sections.title: Build artifacts
sections.body: CI retains all three Astro app outputs and aggregate release evidence as the public-site-build artifact for seven days so reviewers can inspect static HTML and route ownership without treating the artifact as hosted cutover evidence.

sections.title: Claim and safety checks
sections.body: scripts/check-public-repo.mjs rejects secret-like assignments, PAN-like and GSTIN-like identifiers, missing governance files, unsupported policy scope, broken route metadata, incomplete migration-ledger evidence, missing legal governance anchors, missing repository-settings gates, missing contributor-intake guardrails, missing dependency-surface policy, and missing route-cleanup governance prompts.

sections.title: Dependency surface evidence
sections.body: scripts/public-checks/dependency-policy.mjs keeps package.json private, blocks runtime dependencies and lifecycle scripts, reviews the build-tool and exact Sanchika GitHub-artifact devDependency surface, verifies pnpm-lock.yaml against the workspace dependency surface, checks Dependabot groups and reviewers, aligns pnpm@10.28.2 and Node 24 across workflows, and requires every external GitHub Action to be pinned to a reviewed SHA.

sections.title: Contributor intake evidence
sections.body: CODEOWNERS keeps governance, release, policy, route data, rendering, docs, scripts, and package changes owner-reviewed. Issue contact links route security or sensitive-data reports, sensitive conduct reports, and support-boundary questions away from unsafe public issue content.

sections.title: Repository settings evidence
sections.body: scripts/public-checks/repository-settings.mjs pins the documented public repo posture, protected check names, review-gate workflow posture, Pages guard prerequisites, pinned checkout actions, CNAME cutover blocking, and git diff --check alignment across pnpm verify and CI.

sections.title: Legal governance evidence
sections.body: scripts/public-checks/legal-governance.mjs keeps Apache-2.0 source-license terms separate from ComplyEaze-family trademark rights, blocks unsupported government/GSTN/CBIC endorsement claims in content data, and preserves fork/operator clarity.

sections.title: Hosted route evidence
sections.body: scripts/check-hosted-routes.mjs verifies hosted ComplyEaze destination pages, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, canonical tags, metadata, and main landmarks against retained local release evidence. Its summaries explicitly say redirect evidence is not checked by that script and preview hosts do not prove production cutover.

sections.title: Route cleanup governance
sections.body: Route cleanup proposals need route-level ledger rows, rendered /migration/ evidence, hosted route summaries, redirect behavior, rollback evidence, and a separate private-app cleanup PR before any parent ComplyEaze route is removed or redirected. Public evidence pages do not authorize cleanup by themselves.

sections.title: Pages deploy guard
sections.body: The GitHub Pages workflow is readiness-only unless it runs on main with ENABLE_GITHUB_PAGES_DEPLOY set to true. It runs the full verify gate before uploading only apps/complyeaze/dist, and it is not parent-route cleanup evidence without hosted route, canonical, redirect, and rollback review.

sections.title: Review gate
sections.body: The GitHub Review gate is designed to block unresolved review threads and requested-changes states. It complements, but does not replace, rendered review and the manual review-rectify loop.

routes.title: Sanchika Adoption Evidence | ComplyEaze
routes.description: Artifact-backed Sanchika v0.1.0 adoption evidence for the three ComplyEaze public Astro applications.
routes.heading: Sanchika adoption is tied to reviewed release artifacts
routes.summary: ComplyEaze, Axal, and Pack consume the same reviewed Sanchika v0.1.0 GitHub artifact set for a bounded public-shell baseline.

sections.title: Artifact boundary
sections.body: Tokens, primitives, and patterns resolve from the reviewed GitHub release tarballs with recorded checksums and no nested source imports.

sections.title: Consumer boundary
sections.body: P2 adopts semantic shell roles and existing primitive jobs across three public Astro apps. Product-pattern composition remains reserved for P3 craft review.

sections.title: Rollback boundary
sections.body: The complete package set rolls back together to the reviewed v0.0.2 artifacts without deployment, domain, database, or private-application changes.
